items.q: What does 'fusion' mean as a musical term?
items.correct: The integration of elements from different traditions - rhythms, instruments, scales, production - into a coherent new style.
items.misconception: Fusion just means two bands playing their own songs at the same time.
items.random_wrong: Fusion means turning a piece up to maximum volume.
items.why: Fusion works at the level of musical materials: a fused style absorbs features such as clave rhythms or modal melodies, rather than simply juxtaposing finished pieces.

items.q: Is the clave a chord pattern?
items.correct: No - it is a rhythmic timeline, a fixed two-bar pattern that the whole ensemble locks onto; it is also the name of the wooden sticks that play it.
items.misconception: The clave is the chord progression that Cuban songs are built on.
items.random_wrong: The clave is the lead singer in a son band.
items.why: Because pop students think in chord loops, they map 'clave' onto harmony; in Afro-Cuban music the organising loop is rhythmic, not harmonic.

items.q: Did Debussy quote real gamelan pieces in his gamelan-influenced music?
items.correct: No - he evoked the gamelan's sound world through pentatonic writing, layered texture and resonant sonority rather than direct quotation.
items.misconception: Debussy copied actual Javanese gamelan tunes note for note.
items.random_wrong: Debussy hired a gamelan to perform inside the piano.
items.why: Influence is not quotation: Debussy heard gamelan at the Paris Exposition and absorbed its textures and scales into his own idiom, a classic example of cultural fusion.

items.q: In Cuban music, the clave is best described as what?
items.random_wrong: A harmonic sequence of four chords
items.why: It's A repeating two-bar rhythmic pattern. “A type of five-string guitar” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Debussy's piano piece 'Pagodes' evokes the sound of which ensemble?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A Javanese gamelan. “A New Orleans brass band” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the best definition of 'fusion' in music?
items.correct: Blending elements of distinct musical traditions into a new style
items.why: Correct answer: Blending elements of distinct musical traditions into a new style. “Playing two unrelated songs simultaneously” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Courtney Pine, an artist associated with British jazz fusion, is principally a what?
items.why: It's Saxophonist. “Flamenco guitarist” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many strokes are played in one full two-bar cycle of the son clave pattern?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 strokes. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
